source: Exercise 6 Part A: Choose the correct answer
question: Where did the students go for their trip?
passage: Last month, our school organised a field trip to a coastal area to help students learn more about the environment. We left early in the morning and travelled by bus for nearly two hours before arriving at the beach. Everyone was excited because it was a sunny day and the sea looked beautiful. When we got there, our teacher divided us into small groups and gave each group a clipboard, a pen and a task sheet. First, we walked slowly along the coast and carefully observed the surroundings. We noticed that although the beach was beautiful, there was a lot of plastic pollution everywhere. There were plastic bags, bottles and even broken fishing nets lying in the sand. Some students felt upset and surprised because the rubbish was harming the wildlife. Our teacher explained that sea animals such as turtles and fish can mistake plastic for food and get sick or even die. This made us realise how serious the problem was. After that, each group worked together to clean the beach. We collected as much rubbish as we could and put it into large bags. It was hard work, but we felt proud because we were helping to protect the environment. In the afternoon, we sat in a circle and discussed what we had learned. Our teacher talked about simple ways to protect the environment, such as reducing waste, reusing materials and recycling properly. Then, we completed a short survey and shared our ideas with the class. Before going home, we took some photos and enjoyed the fresh air by the sea. It was not only a fun trip but also a meaningful experience because we learned that even small actions can make a big difference.
